column10: Smart farming / IoT solutions, Farm management software, Agri data analytics / AI solutions, Agri-biotech
column11:
HEFT is an AI-powered monitoring system for dairy cows that detects early signs of health problems and fertility cycles using wearable sensors and machine learning.

Problem addressed:
Dairy farmers still rely largely on manual observation to detect illness and fertility cycles. This leads to late treatment, reproductive losses, increased veterinary costs, and reduced productivity.

Technology used:
HEFT combines wearable sensors (collars and internal bolus devices), IoT data transmission, and machine learning algorithms to analyze cow behaviour and physiological signals in real time.

Key differentiators:
Our system focuses on early prediction rather than only activity tracking. It combines multiple sensor types with adaptive AI models trained on farm-specific data.

Advantages:
HEFT enables farmers to detect problems earlier, improve reproductive efficiency, reduce losses, and manage herd health with data-driven decisions.
column13:
Our primary target customers are medium and large dairy farms (typically 200+ cows) that are looking to improve herd health management, reproductive efficiency, and overall productivity through data-driven technologies.

Secondary customers include dairy consultants, veterinarians, and herd management advisors who support farms in optimizing herd performance and decision-making.

We focus on professional dairy operations that are actively investing in precision agriculture and smart farm technologies.
column14:
We are currently operating primarily in Ukraine, where we collaborate with dairy farms and industry partners through our existing PROFEED network.

We are also expanding internationally and already have pilot collaborations and customer interest in several European countries, including Romania, as well as connections with farms and industry partners in the United States and the EU.

Our strategy is to scale first across Europe and North America, where dairy farms actively adopt precision livestock technologies.

column11:
Y-Harvest is an AI-driven soil intelligence platform that combines proprietary IoT GeoStation sensors with cloud-based analytics to deliver real-time agronomic insights and recommendations.

Problem addressed

Agronomic decisions are currently based on:

episodic soil laboratory tests (with 4–7 days latency)

low-density data collection (1–2 measurements per season)

This results in:

high uncertainty in fertilizer application

yield losses due to reactive decision-making

3–4 year experimental cycles for new crops or hybrids

There is no continuous data layer to model cause-effect relationships between soil conditions, weather, and yield.

Technology used

IoT hardware layer

GeoStation (ESP32-based sensor nodes)

Measures: soil moisture, multi-depth soil temperature, EC, microclimate, light

Edge communication via gateway (Wi-Fi Long Range / GSM)

Cloud & data pipeline

AWS IoT Core for ingestion

Time-series storage and spatial mapping

FastAPI-based backend

AI / analytics layer

Proprietary agronomic indices (soil dynamics, gradients, stress indicators)

Machine learning models for field condition prediction

LLM-based reporting using multi-factor analysis (~60+ variables)

Integration of external datasets:

yield maps

soil lab analysis

crop rotation history

Key differentiators

High-frequency data collection

Data captured every 6 hours vs. industry standard of once per season

End-to-end system architecture

Integrated hardware + data + AI (not a standalone tool)

Comparative field analytics

Enables A/B testing across field zones

Built-in R&D capability for agribusinesses

Data integrity approach

Separation of measured vs. derived parameters

Avoids false precision common in agri-AI solutions

Scalable data architecture

Designed for future data monetization (data-as-a-service)

Advantages

Reduced fertilizer costs through optimized timing and application rates

Shortened experimentation cycles (from years to a single season)

Real-time decision-making instead of delayed insights

Plug-and-play deployment with minimal operational overhead

Cost-efficient subscription model (~$10 per station per month), enabling scalable adoption

column11:
PivotHawk in an IoT Monitoring Device for Center Pivot Irrigation Systems

Problem addressed:
Farmers lose up to 30% of crops and spend $500–$1,500/season on manual field inspections due to lack of affordable real-time monitoring for pivot irrigation systems. Undetected malfunctions cause 5–10% water overconsumption and 300+ wasted labor hours per season.

Technology used:
A Plug&Play IoT device (replaces standard light socket in <2 min) with 4G/LTE-M connectivity, GPS/compass positioning, IP67 protection, and OTA firmware updates. Paired with a cloud-based web platform providing real-time RUN/STOP status, fault alerts, and field location tracking.

Key differentiators:
- Does not void irrigation system warranty or leasing agreements
- Universal compatibility with all pivot brands (Lindsay, Reinke, Valley, etc.)
- Installation under 2 minutes — no technician required
- Priced at $495 + $150/year SaaS — 6–14x cheaper than competitors ($3,000–$7,000)

Advantages:
- Only affordable solution (<$500) in a market with no dominant player at this price point
- Saves farmers up to $5,000/year per system in labor, fuel, and prevented crop losses
- Scalable ecosystem roadmap: soil moisture, NDVI cameras, AI-powered field analytics
- Validated in Canada (pilot) and Ukraine; 50+ farms on waitlist across 5 countries
-There are plans to develop an ecosystem comprising several devices
column13: Small-to-large scale farmers and agricultural enterprises operating center pivot irrigation systems, primarily in North America (USA, Canada), Ukraine, and the EU.
column18:
1. Certification & Regulatory compliance
Device must be certified separately for each market - FCC (USA), IC (Canada), CE (EU), and Ukraine. PCT patent filing required across key markets. 

2. Financing
Currently pre-revenue. Hardware production requires upfront capital for component procurement and small-batch manufacturing. Scaling from 10 MVP units to at least 100+/month demands working capital before revenue covers costs.
 4. Distribution & partnerships
Pivot irrigation is a relationship-driven industry. Reaching farmers efficiently requires local agricultural dealers, distributors, and equipment resellers.

5. Localization
Platform and alerts must support local languages, units (metric/imperial), and local agronomic practices. Customer support requires local-language capability. 

6. Market education
Adoption of new solutions requires demonstration programs, pilot partnerships, and trust-building with early adopters.

7. Geopolitical risk
Core R&D team is based in Ukraine (wartime conditions), which creates operational risk and complicates international investor conversations despite the team successfully operating through it.

column11:
Core Product(s) / Service(s)
Feodal is a comprehensive digital ecosystem of products that brings together over 190,000 users, 3,000 farmers, and 600 communities into a unified environment for asset and operations management. Our product stack includes:
Feodal FMS (Farm Management System):
 A fully vertically integrated farm management system. It combines data from IoT devices, satellite analytics, and tools for long-term business model planning. This enables not only equipment monitoring but also modeling seasonal economics and optimizing costs.
Feodal Land & Hromada:
 Audit platforms for farmers and territorial communities. We consolidate data from state registries, enabling communities to detect illegal land use and significantly increase local revenues, while helping farmers compete more effectively.
MOYE (moye.com.ua):
 A mobile application for landowners that simplifies property management. It serves as both a transaction tool (buying and selling) and a control tool (access to state registry data). It enables direct peer-to-peer communication between owners, tenants, and the market, and provides nationwide tenant reputation tracking.
Feodal Demining:
 Specialized solutions and unique expertise for mine action operators, digitizing processes related to contaminated territories.
Landmarket.in.ua:
 A specialized analytical platform for transparent land market monitoring. It collects and visualizes data on every official land sale transaction in Ukraine. This allows investors, agribusinesses, and legal professionals to assess real market land values in any region, track price dynamics, and make informed decisions based on objective data rather than assumptions.

Problem Addressed
We address critical gaps in the agricultural sector:
Economic losses: Due to lack of data transparency, territorial communities and tenants lose potential income.
Fair competition: We create conditions for transparent competition among tenants and system users.
Operational fragmentation: The absence of a unified data window for registries, satellite data, and telematics hinders high-quality long-term planning.
Communication gap: The lack of direct and trusted interaction between market participants (owners, businesses, communities) creates risks and inefficiencies.
And many more.

Technology Used
We build our own ecosystem of cloud services based on advanced development practices and a cloud-native approach. For example, by using Kubernetes as the foundation of our infrastructure, we ensure seamless scaling for thousands of users and reliable processing of large-scale data.
AI R&D & Strategic Partnership:
 Our in-house AI R&D center systematically integrates AI into our products and business processes. In collaboration with KMBS (AI Workshop), we train executives to adopt AI-driven decision-making practices.
IoT & Data Fusion:
 Integration of machinery sensors, satellite imagery, and state registries in real time.

Key Differentiators & Advantages
Ecosystem Resilience:
 We are building a growing network of participants (farmers, communities, mine action operators, landowners), creating a strong network effect.
Decision Intelligence:
 We transform raw data into actionable scenarios that enhance business value and capitalization.
Vision of the Future:
 We believe the future of global agriculture lies in maximum transparency and dense communication within the ecosystem. Our mission is to create an environment where every connection is digital, secure, and mutually beneficial.

column10: Agri data analytics / AI solutions, Farm management software, Smart farming / IoT solutions
column11:
Problem addressed:
US dairy farms lose $180,000+/year per 500 cows from health issues detected too late. By Day 7, when a farmer finally notices something is wrong, treatment costs 5× more and losses are already locked in. Mastitis: $444/case. Missed heat cycle: $300/event. Heat stress in California: $120/cow/month. The tools exist to react — not to predict. That's the gap Howcow fills.
Technology used:
IoT collar sensors paired with our proprietary LAD algorithm — validated by the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine and tested on industrial-scale farms. The system tracks each cow's individual behavioral baseline 24/7 and detects anomalies 8–48 hours before any visible symptom. The farmer gets a phone alert: which cow, what's wrong, what to do. No manual checks. No guesswork.
Key differentiators:
94% accuracy on real farm data. Patent-pending algorithm. Benchmarked against 8 global competitors — the only Howcow combining a 8-48h predictive window, behavioral micro-patterns, NAS-validated science, and disease-specific detection (mastitis, lameness, fertility) in one platform. Not a better tracker — a different generation of technology.
Advantages:
$180k/year recoverable per farm. Zero-risk trial model — no upfront commitment, results visible within weeks. Scientifically defensible IP that holds up with institutional partners and investors alike.
Critically — Howcow is already establishing itself in the US market. We recently presented at DairyCon, are actively preparing our first California pilot, and have initiated partnerships with two US universities. We're on the ground — studying the market, building distributor relationships, and understanding what American dairy farmers actually need. This isn't a company planning to enter the US. We're already here.
column13:
Our primary target is mid-to-large dairy farm operators managing 300–2,000+ cows across the US, where every percentage point of herd health directly impacts revenue. High labor costs, thin margins, and the scale of American dairy operations make early detection not a luxury but a financial necessity.
Secondary targets include farm management companies, veterinary service providers, and agricultural cooperatives that serve multiple farms and benefit from platform-level data across herds.
We also see strong interest from research institutes and universities — our proprietary LAD anomaly detection algorithm has applications well beyond livestock. Institutions working in crop science, defense technology, and behavioral pattern analysis can leverage the same mathematical core for anomaly detection in entirely different domains — from monitoring biological systems to detecting irregularities in complex field environments. We are already in active contact with two US universities exploring this direction.
Longer term, Howcow is built for any operation — agricultural, scientific, or defense-adjacent — where detecting deviation from a normal pattern early translates directly into better outcomes and lower costs
column16: Ninja Acceleration Program, Seeds of Bravery, CES 2025, SelectUSA Investment Summit, DairyCon 2025, CEATEC Japan, CfE Accelerator, Booster Deep Tech Accelerator, EBRD Star Venture
column18:
Scaling a deep-tech hardware+software platform from Ukraine into the US market comes with real challenges — and we're navigating them directly.
Partnerships & market entry: The US dairy industry runs on trust and long-standing relationships. Breaking in requires local credibility — distributors, advisors, and university partners who vouch for you. We've been building this network on the ground, including presence at DairyCon and SelectUSA, and active conversations with two US universities.
Certification & hardware compliance: IoT devices require FCC certification for the US market. We're factoring this into our pilot preparation timeline and working with partners who have experience navigating US hardware compliance.
Financing: As a Ukrainian company operating in wartime, accessing Western financing requires extra groundwork — establishing US legal presence, building investor trust across jurisdictions, and demonstrating traction in-market before institutional capital moves. We're actively working through this.
Localization & mentality: American farmers evaluate technology differently than European ones — ROI has to be immediate and obvious, the onboarding has to be frictionless, and trust is built through peer referrals, not specs. We're adapting our go-to-market and communication accordingly.
The context: We're a wartime startup. That adds complexity — but it also sharpens execution. Every challenge we've faced building this in Ukraine has made the team exceptionally resourceful and resilient.
column19:
Participating in this program is not just about ticking boxes — it's about making an irreversible step into the US market with the right foundation.
Pilot launch & first US contracts. We are already preparing our first farm pilot in the US. The program accelerates this from preparation to execution — with the credibility of UC Berkeley behind us, doors open differently. We expect to leave with at least one signed pilot agreement and the beginning of a real commercial footprint.
Distribution & channel partnerships. The American dairy industry is built on relationships, not cold emails. Being embedded in the Silicon Valley ecosystem for two weeks — meeting operators, investors, and industry insiders — gives us the kind of warm network that would otherwise take years to build. We expect at least one formalized US distribution or channel partnership to come directly from program connections.
Investor relationships for our Seed round. We are targeting a $1M Seed round. The structured pitch sessions and investor access within the program are precisely the environment where meaningful capital conversations begin. Our goal is to leave with active investor relationships — not just business cards.
Mentality shift & market understanding. This may be the most underrated outcome. American farmers think differently. They evaluate technology through ROI speed, peer trust, and frictionless adoption — not feature lists. Six months of mentorship from people who live inside this ecosystem will reshape how we communicate, position, and sell. That mindset recalibration has compounding value far beyond the program itself.
Sustainability & long-term scalability. The post-program mentorship structure — six months, two sessions per month — is exactly what a company at our stage needs: not a one-time event, but a sustained feedback loop as we execute our first pilots, refine our business model, and prepare for scale. We intend to be one of the program's long-term success stories, presented at WINWIN Summit 2026 with measurable results in hand.
Broader impact. Howcow is a Ukrainian deep-tech company, built during wartime, solving a real global problem. Every successful step we take in the US market is proof that Ukrainian innovation is world-class — not despite the circumstances, but because of the resilience they forged. This program is an opportunity to carry that message into Silicon Valley and make it permanent.

Agro Intelligence Platform
1. Briefly about the product
We create a platform for agribusiness that combines data from various databases and documents and other systems and provides simple answers to key business questions.
The platform allows you to:
- see real profit
- analyze yield
- receive answers through AI
- build forecasts for prices and revenues

These are not just dashboards. This is an intellectual layer above the business.

2. Problem
In most agricultural companies, data is scattered: - Excel, BAS or other - agro systems - separate reports
Because of this:
- there is no single truth
- the numbers do not match
- analytics are done manually
- it takes days to get answers
- decisions are made on intuition

The biggest problem is the lack of a single data model.

column18:
AI-powered platform that connects agricultural data and provides insights, answers, and forecasts.
Problem
Agricultural data is fragmented, making it difficult to understand profitability and make fast decisions.
Solution
A unified data platform with semantic layer and AI that delivers instant insights and forecasts.
Value
Faster decisions, clear profitability, and better planning.
Business Model
Subscription-based SaaS and enterprise licensing.


As we scale internationally, we face several key challenges across multiple dimensions:

1. Regulatory & Compliance
Different countries have varying regulations related to data privacy, agricultural standards, and AI usage. Ensuring compliance (e.g., GDPR in Europe or local data residency laws) requires legal adaptation and operational flexibility.

2. Data Standardization & Integration
Agricultural data is highly fragmented and differs by region (formats, sources, quality). Integrating local datasets into a unified semantic layer while maintaining accuracy and consistency is a significant technical challenge.

3. Localization & Market Adaptation
Each market has unique agricultural practices, crops, climate conditions, and user expectations. The platform must be localized not only linguistically but also in terms of insights, models, and UX relevance.

4. Partnerships & Ecosystem Development
Scaling requires strong local partnerships (agri-tech providers, cooperatives, government bodies, data providers). Building trust and aligning incentives across stakeholders takes time and effort.

5. Go-to-Market Strategy & Sales Cycles
Enterprise and agricultural clients often have long decision-making cycles. Establishing credibility in new markets and adapting sales strategies to local dynamics is critical.

6. Financing & Scaling Resources
International expansion requires significant investment in product adaptation, sales, and operations. Balancing growth with sustainable unit economics is a key challenge, especially for a bootstrapped or early-stage SaaS.

7. AI Model Adaptation & Accuracy
AI models must be trained and validated on region-specific data (soil, weather, crops), which impacts the accuracy of forecasts and recommendations.
column19:
I expect several concrete, measurable outcomes from participating in the program:

1. Strategic Partnerships
Establish 2–3 partnerships with agri-enterprises, data providers, or technology partners in the U.S. and EU markets to accelerate data integration and market entry.

2. Pilot Launches & Market Validation
Launch 1–2 pilot projects with international clients (e.g., farms, agri-holdings, or agri-tech companies) to validate product-market fit in new regions and collect real-world performance data.

3. Investment Readiness & Fundraising
Refine our investment strategy, pitch, and financial model with the goal of raising a pre-seed/seed round from international investors following the program.

4. Product & AI Model Enhancement
Adapt our AI models and data architecture to better support region-specific agricultural conditions (crops, climate, regulations), improving forecast accuracy and decision support capabilities.

5. Go-to-Market & Scaling Strategy
Develop a clear and actionable international go-to-market strategy, including ICP definition, pricing model validation, and scalable sales channels.

6. Brand Positioning & Global Visibility
Strengthen our positioning as a data-driven AgriTech platform and increase visibility within the global innovation ecosystem, particularly in the U.S.

7. Export Growth
Initiate first international revenue streams through signed contracts or LOIs with clients outside Ukraine.

column11:
Valvix is an IoT-based greenhouse and farm automation system that enables agricultural businesses to monitor and control all critical operations in real time — from climate and irrigation to boiler management, warehouses, and workforce coordination.
Problem addressed:
Most greenhouse and farm operations in Ukraine and emerging markets rely on manual control — adjusting heating, ventilation, and irrigation based on intuition, and discovering problems only after crop damage has occurred. Combined with Ukraine’s unstable power grid and frequent blackouts, farmers face energy waste (up to 30-40% overspending on heating), crop losses from undetected climate failures, and inability to operate during power outages.
Technology used:
Valvix combines custom IoT hardware nodes built on ESP32 microcontrollers with a cloud-based monitoring and control platform. Sensors continuously track air and soil temperature, humidity, soil moisture, weather conditions, and plant health indicators. Our intelligent control algorithms automatically manage heating systems (boilers, floor heating, radiators, fan heaters), ventilation (motorized windows), irrigation, lighting, and energy consumption. When connected to the internet, AI-powered cloud services optimize climate, irrigation, and resource management. The system also operates fully offline via a local edge server, and even a single standalone node can execute basic automation rules independently — ensuring zero downtime under any conditions.
Key differentiators:
        ∙        Easy integration and fast deployment: the system is designed to plug into existing farm infrastructure with minimal setup time
        ∙        Predictive alerts: real-time notifications for critical issues — overheating, heating failures (pipe bursts, insufficient capacity), and early detection of plant disease risks before visible symptoms appear
        ∙        Three-tier resilience: full cloud AI when online → local server when internet is lost → autonomous node operation when everything else is down
        ∙        Smart energy management: integrates with solar panels and wind turbines, automatically activating energy-intensive equipment (motors, heaters) when generation is high and reducing load when it drops
        ∙        Battle-tested during Ukrainian blackouts: successfully managed hydroponic vertical farms running on batteries and solar, covering 95% of energy needs without generators — activating backup generators only as a last resort
        ∙        Affordable and scalable: designed for small and mid-size farms, not just large agro-holdings — start with monitoring, scale to full automation
Advantages:
        ∙        Energy savings of 20-35% through intelligent heating, ventilation, and energy load management
        ∙        Complete farm management: climate, irrigation, soil analysis, weather monitoring, boiler control, warehouse and personnel management
        ∙        Real-time dashboards accessible via web, tablet (kiosk mode), and mobile
        ∙        Built and tested on our own 4.5 ha greenhouse farm — we are both the developer and the end user
        ∙        Proven in real production conditions with 100+ clients across Ukraine
column13:
Our primary target customers are small and mid-size greenhouse farms (from 100 m² to 5 ha) growing vegetables, herbs, and seedlings — operations that need professional-grade yet easy-to-deploy automation.
Secondary segments include:
        ∙        Indoor and vertical hydroponic farms
        ∙        Nurseries and seedling producers
        ∙        Agricultural enterprises managing multiple greenhouses and boiler systems
        ∙        Farm operators looking to integrate renewable energy (solar, wind) into their production cycle
Geography: Ukraine (current market), with expansion plans to the United States, Middle East (UAE), and Eastern Europe.

column18:
Regulatory & Certification: Understanding and complying with local agricultural regulations, data privacy laws, and electrical safety standards in target markets (US, EU, UAE). Each country may have different requirements for commercial IoT devices used in agricultural settings.
Financing: As a bootstrapped company, we lack the capital reserves needed for rapid international expansion — establishing local presence, attending trade shows, building demo installations, and supporting early customers in new markets requires significant upfront investment.
Partnerships: Finding reliable local partners — distributors, integrators, and agronomists — who understand both greenhouse operations and IoT technology in each target market. Without local partners, customer support and installation across time zones becomes a challenge.
Localization: Adapting the platform (UI, alerts, documentation) to local languages and agricultural practices. Greenhouse growing methods, crop calendars, heating/cooling priorities, and even sensor placement differ significantly between Ukrainian, American, and Middle Eastern markets.
Market awareness: Valvix is well-known in Ukraine’s greenhouse community, but has zero brand recognition internationally. Building trust with foreign farmers who are unfamiliar with Ukrainian AgriTech requires a different go-to-market approach.
column19:
Market validation in the US: Conduct structured customer discovery with American greenhouse operators to validate product-market fit, understand their specific pain points, and adapt Valvix features to the US market requirements.
Pilot launches: Establish 2-3 pilot installations at US-based greenhouse farms to demonstrate Valvix capabilities in real production conditions and collect feedback for market-specific improvements.
Strategic partnerships: Sign distribution or integration agreements with US-based agricultural technology distributors, greenhouse equipment suppliers, or farm management companies who can serve as local sales and support channels.
Investor connections: Build relationships with Silicon Valley and US-based investors focused on AgriTech and IoT to secure seed or pre-Series A funding ($200K-$500K) for international expansion.
Export strategy: Develop a clear go-to-market roadmap for the US, including pricing model, support infrastructure, and regulatory compliance plan — ready for execution within 6 months after the program.
Knowledge and mentorship: Gain practical insights into the US agricultural market landscape, business culture, and scaling strategies from experienced mentors and Berkeley faculty, which we can also apply to other international markets (UAE, EU).
Brand credibility: Leverage the UC Berkeley and AGRO Ukraine program affiliation to increase Valvix visibility and credibility in international markets — having a Silicon Valley stamp of approval is a significant trust factor for potential customers and investors.

column11:
PROFEED is a digital feed management platform for dairy farms that automates feeding processes, ensures accuracy, and provides real-time operational insights.

Problem addressed:
Dairy farms lose significant money due to inaccurate feed loading, human errors, and lack of control over daily operations. Feeding is the largest cost (up to 70%), yet it is often poorly monitored and managed.

Technology used:
PROFEED combines proprietary IoT hardware (weighing terminals installed on feed mixers), mobile applications for operators, and a cloud-based management platform with analytics. The system works both online and offline, ensuring стабильність even in low-connectivity environments.

Key differentiators:

Full control of the actual feeding process, not just planning
Offline-first functionality (critical for farms)
Integrated hardware + software solution
Strong traction: 750+ farms, ~47% market share in Ukraine
Proven results in real farm conditions

Advantages:

Up to 11% increase in farm profitability
5–10% reduction in feed losses
Elimination of human errors in feeding
Real-time visibility for farm managers
Easy adoption with minimal disruption to daily operations

PROFEED focuses not just on collecting data, but on improving real farm management and delivering measurable economic results

column11:
"Problem addressed:
Large agroholdings managing over 1,000 hectares rely on manual soil sampling typically performed once per month, which does not provide sufficient temporal resolution for decision-making. There is no scalable solution for deploying dense soil sensor networks across large fields. As a result, irrigation is often unregulated, leading to up to 40% water waste, while non-optimal hydrothermal conditions can cause up to 20% yield loss.

Technology used: 
IoT-based soil sensor networks (LoRaWAN) for real-time field data. Satellite imagery (e.g., Sentinel-2) for vegetation monitoring.  Weather data integration.  Machine learning models.

Key differentiators: 
Early prediction of crop stress 3–7 days in advance.  Combined use of IoT + satellite + weather data in a single platform.  Focus on real-time, field-level monitoring, not only historical analytics.  Scalable architecture suitable for large agricultural enterprises

Advantages: 
Hourly automated soil-moisture and temperature data. Scalable wireless IoT sensors network. Preventive stress alerts 3-7 days before visible damage. Increase yield by up to 20%, reduce water consumption by up to 40% and Increase profits by up to €150/ha·year."

column11:
"GISPoint is a cloud-based GeoAI platform designed for large-scale agricultural operations and agribusiness ecosystems.

We solve the problem of fragmented data, complex land management, and slow decision-making across thousands of hectares by combining geospatial data, satellite imagery, and operational systems into a single intelligent platform.

Our technology integrates GIS, AI/ML, and cloud infrastructure to automate key processes such as:

management zone generation,
vegetation and yield analysis (NDVI/SBI),
integration of agronomic, operational, and satellite data,
spatial dashboards for real-time decision-making.

Unlike generic GIS tools, GISPoint is built specifically for agriculture — enabling companies like MHP to:

centralise all land, crop, and operational data in one system,
automate agronomic analytics and reduce manual processes,
improve accuracy of planning and resource allocation,
gain full visibility across the entire land bank.

The platform supports use cases such as precision farming, agrochemical planning, satellite monitoring, and integration with ERP and data platforms like AgroDataHub.

Result: faster, data-driven decisions, reduced operational complexity, and scalable digital infrastructure for modern agribusiness."
column13:
The target customers are professionals and organizations responsible for designing, operating, or optimizing buildings and controlled environments where efficiency, comfort, and reliability are critical. This includes system integrators, electrical contractors, and developers who implement smart automation in residential and commercial projects, as well as hotel owners and operators seeking to improve guest experience while reducing operational costs through energy-efficient automation.

In addition, the solution is highly relevant for industrial users and agricultural businesses - particularly greenhouse operators - who require precise environmental control, automation of routine processes, and centralized management of multiple systems. End users such as homeowners also benefit as a secondary audience, gaining intuitive control over lighting, climate, security, and energy usage, but the primary focus remains on B2B partners and businesses that deploy and scale these systems.

We expect the program to accelerate our entry into international AgriTech markets through validated partnerships, pilot projects, and investor engagement. Concretely, we aim to secure at least 3-5 pilot deployments of our smart greenhouse automation system with commercial farms or greenhouse operators, as well as establish strategic partnerships with local integrators or distributors. In parallel, we plan to refine our market positioning and go-to-market strategy based on customer discovery and mentorship, enabling us to convert these pilots into long-term commercial contracts and export agreements.

The program will also help us adapt our product to U.S. regulatory and market requirements, strengthen our business model, and prepare for scalable growth. Ultimately, we aim to leave the program with validated demand in a new market, signed partnership agreements, and a clear pathway to revenue growth through international sales.

column11:
"Almexoft is a no-code/low-code platform designed to automate business processes and document workflows mainly for mid-size and enterprise companies.

Problem addressed:

Many organizations rely on fragmented systems, manual processes, and email-based approvals, leading to delays, lack of transparency, compliance risks, and operational inefficiencies, especially in document-heavy environments such as procurement, finance, HR, and supply chain.

Technology used:

Almexoft is built on a modular, package-based architecture that enables flexible development and deployment of functional components. The platform leverages a modern enterprise technology stack, including Java for backend logic, JavaScript and React for the user interface, and supports cross-platform mobile development (iOS/Android via Swift and Java).

It operates on a scalable infrastructure compatible with both Windows and Linux environments and supports major enterprise-grade databases such as Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, and PostgreSQL. The platform includes its own data management layer (ORM) and provides open API capabilities for seamless integration with external systems.

Additionally, the platform can be deployed both on-premise within a customer’s infrastructure and in cloud environments. Almexoft is cloud-agnostic, allowing deployment across leading cloud providers such as Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, or other private and public cloud environments..

Key differentiators:

Rapid implementation (ranging from a few weeks to a number of months, as opposed to traditional enterprise systems)
No-code approach enabling “citizen development” without heavy IT involvement
Strong focus on document-centric processes and compliance
Flexible architecture independent from large vendor ecosystems (e.g., Microsoft, SAP)

Advantages:

Up to 80% reduction in implementation costs compared to traditional solutions
Faster process execution (often 2× improvement in document processing time)
Full visibility and control over workflows and approvals
Scalable solution adaptable across multiple departments and industries"
column13: Almexoft is designed for medium and large enterprises whose operations involve complex processes and large volumes of documentation. This includes organizations that require structured document management workflows, transparency, and regulatory compliance across departments such as operations, finance, procurement, legal, and human resources.
